chapter_summary: This chapter synthesizes the book's core argument that the failure of American education stems from an ideological adherence to Romantic-progressive ideas which contradict empirical reality. To restore effectiveness, the author argues for a pragmatic shift toward content-rich, evidence-based practices that prioritize factual knowledge, hard work, and external incentives over naturalistic, child-centered slogans.

claims.text: The direct origin of the dominant and ineffectual American educational orientation was the progressive movement of the 1920s that emanated from Teachers College, Columbia University.
claims.source_passage: To provide historical perspective, the book has devoted a number of pages to showing that the direct origin of our dominant (and ineffectual) educational orientation was the progressive movement of the 1920s that emanated from Teachers College, Columbia University.

claims.text: The progressive movement is part of the tradition of American Romanticism that began in the early nineteenth century.
claims.source_passage: More broadly, I have placed the progressive movement within the tradition of American Romanticism, which began in the early nineteenth century and has persisted powerfully in our culture ever since.

claims.text: Persistent educational Romanticism thwarts a balanced educational approach that would emphasize high standards, book learning, and hard work in school.
claims.source_passage: It is this pervasive, deep-dyed Romanticism, not just its one-time expression in the progressive movement, which continues to thwart a balanced educational approach that would emphasize high standards, book learning, and hard work in school.

claims.text: The primary issue facing American education is practical effectiveness rather than philosophical or ideological preference.
claims.source_passage: That is certainly part of my message, but to overstress it would misleadingly suggest that the main issue is philosophical or ideological. On the contrary, the main issue is practical.

claims.text: Educational Romanticism is wrong in its empirical assumptions and hence ineffectual in practice.
claims.source_passage: The strongest case against it lies not in the opinion that it is wrong in its ideology but in the fact that it is wrong in its empirical assumptions, and hence ineffectual in practice.

claims.text: Enlightenment principles have proved to be more valid than Romantic principles as guides to educational practice.
claims.source_passage: In the sphere of poetry, Romanticism is arguably superior to the Enlightenment, but in the sphere of education, Romantic principles have proved to be much less valid than Enlightenment ones as guides to practice.

claims.text: There is not a single example in research literature of the successful implementation of progressivist methods in a carefully controlled longitudinal study.
claims.source_passage: But the research literature offers not one example of successful implementation of progressivist methods in a carefully controlled longitudinal study.

claims.text: Process-outcome research consistently shows that the Romantic-progressive approach is the least effective educational approach studied.
claims.source_passage: In fact, as I showed in Chapter 4, process-outcome research has consistently shown just the opposite, that the Romantic-progressive approach is always the least effective approach studied.

claims.text: 'Knowing how to learn' is an abstract skill that does not even exist.
claims.source_passage: We must not accept the claim that knowing how to learn (which is an abstract skill that does not even exist) is more important than having a broad foundation of factual knowledge that really does enable further learning.

claims.text: A broad foundation of factual knowledge is what actually enables further learning.
claims.source_passage: We must not accept the claim that knowing how to learn (which is an abstract skill that does not even exist) is more important than having a broad foundation of factual knowledge that really does enable further learning.

claims.text: Verbal learning is an authentic component of human understanding, contrary to Romantic premises.
claims.source_passage: We must reject the disparagement of verbal learning and the celebration of “hands-on” learning, based on the false Romantic premise that mere words are inauthentic components of human understanding.

claims.text: Adequate schooling is primarily a function of hard work rather than individual talent.
claims.source_passage: We cannot afford still to accept the untrue belief that adequate schooling is natural and painless, and mainly a function of individual talent rather than hard work.

claims.text: Delaying learning until a child is 'ready' does not speed up learning in the long run.
claims.source_passage: We must reject the false claim that delaying learning until the child is “ready” will speed up learning in the long run.

claims.text: Effective learning requires an admixture of external incentive alongside inward interest.
claims.source_passage: We must cease listening to the siren call that learning should be motivated entirely by inward love of the subject and interest in it, without a significant admixture of external incentive.

claims.text: Education should not be motivated entirely by inward love of the subject and interest without a significant admixture of external incentive.
claims.source_passage: It is also assumed that the student’s learning should be motivated entirely by inward love of the subject and interest in it, without a significant admixture of external incentive.

claims.text: The educational reforms proposed in the 1990s are a continuation of Romantic ideas from the 1920s, 30s, and 40s.
claims.source_passage: In short, we must cease attending to the Romantic ideas that the reformers of the 1990s, echoing the reformers of the 1920s, ’30s, and ’40s and all the decades in between, have been pronouncing in chorus.

claims.text: The dominant ideas of the Progressive Thoughtworld are not reforms but are the controlling ideas of failed schools.
claims.source_passage: These ideas are emphatically not reforms. They are the long-dominant controlling ideas of our failed schools.

claims.text: Americans accept progressive educational ideas because they conform to Romantic assumptions about the superiority of the natural over the artificial.
claims.source_passage: Those ideas fall on receptive ears among teachers and Americans generally because of their conformity with our Romantic assumptions about the superiority of the natural over the artificial.

claims.text: The naturalistic approach to education fails to motivate all students equally.
claims.source_passage: One reason they failed was that the naturalistic approach turns out not to motivate all students equally.

claims.text: The naturalistic approach to education does not inculcate knowledge and skill effectively, securely, or universally.
claims.source_passage: More important, it does not inculcate knowledge and skill effectively, securely, or universally.

claims.text: The absence of explicit focus and definite goals in schooling results in an absence of secure and universal learning.
claims.source_passage: Wherever there is an absence of explicit focus and definite goals—that is, wherever there is an absence of “traditional” schooling—there is also an absence of secure and universal learning.

claims.text: Process-outcome research indicates that naturalistic, integrated learning produces highly variable and uncertain results.
claims.source_passage: Process-outcome research has shown that what children bring away from naturalistic, “integrated” learning is likely to be highly variable and uncertain.

claims.text: Educational formalism is the belief that acquiring formal tools is more important than learning specific content.
claims.source_passage: By “formalism” I mean the belief that the particular content which is learned in school (the content I have called “intellectual capital”) is far less important than acquiring the formal tools which will enable a person to learn future content.

claims.text: Educational naturalism is the belief that education is a natural process that is most effective when connected to real-life goals and settings.
claims.source_passage: By the second term, “naturalism,” I mean the belief that education is a natural process with its own inherent forms and rhythms, which may vary with each child, and is most effective when it is connected with natural, real-life goals and settings.

claims.text: Educational formalism and naturalism are half-truths—the most pernicious kinds of errors because they appear so plausible.
claims.source_passage: Both formalism and naturalism are half-truths—the most pernicious kinds of errors because they appear so plausible.

claims.text: Educational formalism is in error in purely practical terms because an emphasis on process and skills rather than content does not result in significantly improved formal skills for students.
claims.source_passage: Educational formalism is in error in purely practical terms, because an emphasis on process and skills rather than on content does not in fact result in significantly improved formal skills for students.

claims.text: The real-life competencies people need, including reading and math, have major components that are domain-specific.
claims.source_passage: The real-life competencies that people need, such as the abilities to read, to write, to communicate, to learn, to analyze, and to grasp and manipulate mathematical symbols, have major components that psychologists have found to be “domain-specific.”

claims.text: Critical thinking skills in one domain do not translate to other domains.
claims.source_passage: This means that an ability to think critically about chess does not translate into an ability to think critically about sailing.

claims.text: An ability to think critically about one domain, such as chess, does not translate into an ability to think critically about another, such as sailing.
claims.source_passage: This means that an ability to think critically about chess does not translate into an ability to think critically about sailing.

claims.text: Expertness in reading partly depends upon the automation of repeated operations through practice, which frees the conscious mind for critical thought.
claims.source_passage: It is true that certain operations of reading are the same from one task to another, so that expertness in reading partly depends upon the automation (through a great deal of practice) of those repeated operations, thus freeing the conscious mind for critical thought.

claims.text: Competent reading about a specific topic depends upon the acquisition of the relevant vocabulary, conventions, and schemas that form the relevant knowledge base.
claims.source_passage: But competent reading about the Civil War also depends upon the acquisition of the relevant vocabulary, conventions, and schemas that form the relevant knowledge base for reading and learning about the Civil War.

claims.text: There is no substitute for requisite domain-specific content knowledge in the performance of reading or any other intellectual skill.
claims.source_passage: There is no substitute for this requisite domain-specific content knowledge in the performance of reading or any other intellectual skill.

claims.text: Schools should not and cannot teach all-purpose reading, thinking, and learning skills.
claims.source_passage: It is a fallacy, then, to claim that the schools should or could teach all-purpose reading, thinking, and learning skills.

claims.text: Adequate attention to the transmission of broad general knowledge paradoxically leads to the possession of general intellectual skills.
claims.source_passage: But paradoxically, adequate attention to the transmission of broad general knowledge actually does lead to general intellectual skills.

claims.text: The American emphasis on formal skills has resulted in students who are deficient in formal skills.
claims.source_passage: Our emphasis on formal skills has resulted in students who are deficient in formal skills

claims.text: An appropriate emphasis on transmitting knowledge results in students who possess skills such as critical thinking and learning to learn.
claims.source_passage: whereas an appropriate emphasis on transmitting knowledge results in students who actually possess the skills that are sought by American educators—skills such as critical thinking and learning to learn.

claims.text: The formalistic model of applying a pattern to a new problem represents the slow, unreliable approach of amateurs and novices rather than experts.
claims.source_passage: It represents the slow, unreliable approach of amateurs and novices rather than the knowledge-informed approach of experts.

claims.text: The formalistic approach to skill results in incompetent performance.
claims.source_passage: It is an approach that results in incompetent performance.

claims.text: A general skill is based on wide-ranging general knowledge that allows a person to apply domain-relevant knowledge to new problems.
claims.source_passage: A general skill, by contrast, is based only partly on habituation to the formal, repeated aspects of the skill (such as word recognition in reading); it is also based on wide-ranging general knowledge.

claims.text: Knowledge of factual relationships regarding a topic is a better foundation for critical reading than an armory of formal procedures.
claims.source_passage: For instance, having as part of one’s general intellectual capital some key factual relationships regarding the Civil War is a much better foundation for skilled, critical reading about the Civil War than an armory of formal procedures.

claims.text: The best foundation for general skill is the inculcation of broad knowledge rather than abstract strategies.
claims.source_passage: The best foundation for general skill is not the inculcation of abstract strategies but the inculcation of broad knowledge.

claims.text: Educational naturalism holds that learning is a process of inherent unfolding that should follow a natural process like the growth of a plant.
claims.source_passage: The second fundamental error of the current doctrine, educational naturalism, holds that the progress of learning is like the growth of a tree or flower, a process that should follow its own proper and inherent unfolding—ideally within a context that is like “life itself.”

claims.text: Americans are culturally predisposed to believe in the rightness of the natural and the harmfulness of the artificial.
claims.source_passage: We Americans are predisposed to believe in the rightness of the natural and the harmfulness of the artificial.

claims.text: Native-language learning is an inexorable and inherent process mediated by dedicated locations in the brain.
claims.source_passage: Scientific opinion has converged on the view that native-language learning is mediated by dedicated locations in the brain, and is as inexorable and inherent as physical development.

claims.text: Artificial interventions, like parental encouragement, are important even in the 'natural' learning of language.
claims.source_passage: Advantaged toddlers, with lots of encouragement and stimulation from their parents, usually become more proficient in language than disadvantaged children, showing that “artificial” interventions are important even in this universal learning.

claims.text: The adequacy of the naturalistic educational tradition hinges on the premise that school learnings are analogous to natural processes like physiomotor development and learning a mother tongue.
claims.source_passage: But the adequacy of that naturalistic tradition hinges on the truth or error of the premise that school learnings are analogous to natural processes like physiomotor development and the learning of the mother tongue.

claims.text: Many learnings to be gained in school are inherently unnatural attainments.
claims.source_passage: if, on the contrary, many learnings to be gained in school are inherently unnatural attainments, just as Emerson complained, then the naturalistic approach would not be self-evidently the best approach to achieving them.

claims.text: Nonnatural educational goals are best attained, and perhaps only attained, through nonnatural means.
claims.source_passage: It might be the case that such nonnatural goals are best attained, perhaps only to be attained, through nonnatural means.

claims.text: The whole-language approach assumes that learning to read is a 'psycholinguistic guessing game' analogous to primary language acquisition.
claims.source_passage: Learning to read is to be understood as ‐a psycholinguistic guessing game‐ on the analogy of primary language acquisition.

claims.text: Alphabetic literacy is not a natural learning analogous to oral language acquisition.
claims.source_passage: This is strong prima facie evidence that literacy is not a natural learning analogous to oral language acquisition

claims.text: A nonnaturalistic approach, including direct instruction in letter-sound correspondences, is the most effective approach to the teaching of reading.
claims.source_passage: A nonnaturalistic approach, including direct instruction in letter-sound correspondences, is by far the most effective approach to the teaching of reading.

claims.text: California's adoption of the whole-language approach led to the state's reading scores sinking to the bottom of national surveys.
claims.source_passage: The state of California unwittingly performed a vast confirmation of the research regarding educational naturalism when it insisted that reading instruction be conducted through the whole-language approach. Subsequently, on the next national survey, California had sunk to the bottom in reading scores.

claims.text: American students score lowest in mathematics compared to all other subjects in international comparisons.
claims.source_passage: even mathematics, where American students score lowest of all subjects in international comparisons, is to be taught by naturalistic methods

claims.text: Psychological and developmental assumptions behind naturalistic dogmas in mathematics are incorrect and lead to poor results.
claims.source_passage: Professor David Geary, in an important series of studies, has shown that the psychological and developmental assumptions behind these naturalistic dogmas are as incorrect in mathematics as they are in reading, and lead to equally poor results.

claims.text: Researches into neurophysiology and cognitive skills converge on fundamental psychological principles that cannot be circumvented.
claims.source_passage: Researches into the neurophysiology of learning and the structure of cognitive skills converge on a few fundamental psychological principles. These foundational principles can be actualized in different ways, but they cannot be circumvented.

claims.text: Educational professionals have selectively used and distorted scientific research to justify progressivist ideology.
claims.source_passage: But the references have been selective and have often ignored inconvenient mainstream findings. Progressivism’s greatest distortion of the scientific consensus may be the use of scientific-sounding terms like ‐developmentally appropriate‐ to justify deferment or neglect of diligence

claims.text: The foundational principles of effective education cannot be circumvented.
claims.source_passage: these foundational principles can be actualized in different ways, but they cannot be circumvented.

claims.text: Progressive educational professionals have used the rhetoric of science selectively while ignoring inconvenient mainstream findings.
claims.source_passage: the rhetoric of educational professionals has bristled with references to “current research.” But the references have been selective and have often ignored inconvenient mainstream findings.

claims.text: Progressivism uses terms like 'developmentally appropriate' to justify the neglect of diligence and factual knowledge despite scientific evidence to the contrary.
claims.source_passage: Progressivism’s greatest distortion of the scientific consensus may be the use of scientific-sounding terms like “developmentally appropriate” to justify deferment or neglect of diligence, or derogation of factual knowledge, while the mainstream scientific community has come to very different conclusions.

claims.text: Human children possess an innate learning instinct necessary for survival.
claims.source_passage: children who lacked the learning instinct would not have been likely to survive.

claims.text: Primary learnings, such as speaking and basic spatial orientation, follow a universal, transcultural sequence determined by human evolution.
claims.source_passage: These natural learnings are based on (though not limited to) primary processes determined by human evolution. These “primary learnings” seem to follow a definite and universal sequence.

claims.text: Secondary learnings, including reading, writing, and base-ten arithmetic, are not universal and do not develop automatically.
claims.source_passage: These secondary learnings do not fit the naturalistic scheme of automatic development. It is quite misleading to think of them on the analogy of an acorn developing into an oak.

claims.text: Secondary learnings do not develop unless they are explicitly taught.
claims.source_passage: The learnings do not develop at all unless they are taught.

claims.text: There is no specific age when a child is 'developmentally ready' by nature to learn reading or arithmetic.
claims.source_passage: there is no age when a child is developmentally ready by nature to learn reading, writing, and arithmetic.

claims.text: A child's readiness for secondary processes depends on prior relevant learning rather than biological development.
claims.source_passage: Children’s readiness for secondary processes is not a matter of natural development but of prior relevant learning. Learning builds on learning.

claims.text: The message of nature regarding most learning is 'the earlier the better.'
claims.source_passage: What nature is really saying about much learning much of the time is “the earlier the better.”

claims.text: Learning requires repeated practice and effort to forge new neural paths; it is not effortless or automatic.
claims.source_passage: On the contrary, scientists have found that it takes repeated practice to forge new neural paths in our brains. Learning involves effort, whether through unconscious play or conscious diligence.

claims.text: Distributed practice is the best approach for achieving retention in long-term memory.
claims.source_passage: Memory studies indicate that the best approach to achieving retention in long-term memory is “distributed practice,” with rehearsal occurring at moderately distant intervals.

claims.text: Intellectual competencies are described, at least operationally, by schema theory.
claims.source_passage: that these competencies are described, at least operationally, by schema theory—a schema being a remembered system of typical traits that are related to each other in typical but adjustable ways.

claims.text: Reading skill requires both automated operational 'rules' and schemas consisting of interconnected knowledge frames.
claims.source_passage: Reading skill, for example, includes both “rules,” that is, mastery of operations continually repeated to the point of automaticity, and schemas consisting of interconnected frames of knowledge.

claims.text: Intellectual skills must rest on rules and schemas because of the physiological limitations of working memory.
claims.source_passage: That intellectual skills should rest on rules and schemas arises from a fundamental limitation of the mind called “short-term,” or “working,” memory, which is capable of retaining and manipulating only a very limited number of items over a very brief time span before degenerating.

claims.text: Automation and chunking are the mental techniques used to overcome working memory constraints.
claims.source_passage: For overcoming these limitations of working memory, the mind uses rules and schemas to achieve automation and chunking.

claims.text: Chunking and schema development are more important than procedural automation for achieving high levels of intellectual skill.
claims.source_passage: In the long run, chunking, which is connected with schema development, is more important for developing very high levels of intellectual skill.

claims.text: Higher levels of reading expertise reside almost entirely in expanding the reader's accessible intellectual capital rather than further rule automation.
claims.source_passage: In reading, for example, once a plateau of rule automation has been attained, higher levels of expertise reside almost entirely in developing, expanding, and making accessible a reader’s relevant intellectual capital.

claims.text: The common educational assumption that a skill is an all-purpose tool applicable to any content is false.
claims.source_passage: But the traditional educational parlance assumes that a skill is an all-purpose tool that can apply to new contents and tasks. Cognitive science shows that, on the contrary, intellectual skills consist of both formal operations and concrete schemas.

claims.text: Formal procedures cannot be effectively applied to unfamiliar problems in unfamiliar domains.
claims.source_passage: People are rarely able to apply formal procedures in appropriate ways to unfamiliar problems that belong to unfamiliar domains.

claims.text: Learning is cumulative, following the principle that 'it takes knowledge to make knowledge.'
claims.source_passage: Knowledge gradually builds on knowledge; the principle behind intellectual capital is that it takes knowledge to make knowledge.

claims.text: Early educational advantages or deficits tend to snowball into large deficiencies or strengths in later life.
claims.source_passage: Small early deficits tend to become large deficiencies in later life; conversely, small initial advantages tend to grow into large ones later.

claims.text: The primary cause of the 'snowball effect' in achievement is the cumulative character of learning itself.
claims.source_passage: But the chief cause is simply the cumulative character of learning itself. The more one knows, the more readily one learns something new.

claims.text: Possessing background knowledge reduces the number of new elements the mind must process, preventing working memory overload.
claims.source_passage: The first advantage is that there are fewer new things which have to be learned. As work on chess expertise has shown, the expert’s possession of well-established schemas reduces the number of new elements that the mind must attend to, helping to prevent the overloading of the expert learner’s short-term memory.

claims.text: A broad base of knowledge provides analogies and categories (cognitive hooks) that make assimilating new learning easier.
claims.source_passage: the broader the base of relevant knowledge, the greater the number of potential analogies or categories available for assimilating new learnings. This increase in cognitive hooks (a kind of mental Velcro) gives educated adults a learning advantage over young children

claims.text: Possessing relevant background knowledge reduces the number of new things that must be learned during a task.
claims.source_passage: The first advantage is that there are fewer new things which have to be learned.

claims.text: The possession of well-established schemas helps prevent the overloading of a learner's short-term memory.
claims.source_passage: the expert’s possession of well-established schemas reduces the number of new elements that the mind must attend to, helping to prevent the overloading of the expert learner’s short-term memory.

claims.text: Novices experience slow progress and constant re-tries because they must keep too many new elements in mind at once, overloading short-term memory.
claims.source_passage: By contrast, the novice, who has to keep many more new elements in mind at once, may quickly overload his short-term memory and experience constant re-trys and slow progress.

claims.text: Educated adults possess a learning advantage over young children due to intellectual capital, despite children potentially having superior raw processing ability.
claims.source_passage: This increase in cognitive hooks (a kind of mental Velcro) gives educated adults a learning advantage over young children, who, in raw processing ability, may far outshine them.

claims.text: Basic principles of learning, including the need for effort, practice, automation, and the cumulative nature of learning, apply universally to all individuals regardless of social status.
claims.source_passage: These few basic principles of learning apply universally: the distinction between primary and secondary learnings, the importance of early beginnings, the need for effort and practice, the need to automate operations and develop relevant intellectual capital to overcome the limitations of short-term memory, and the cumulative nature of learning.

claims.text: Teachers should be granted freedom in how they apply learning principles as long as they achieve good results.
claims.source_passage: That is a good argument for giving teachers a lot of freedom, so long as they get good results.

claims.text: In large educational systems, the effective and fair ways to apply learning principles are relatively narrow.
claims.source_passage: Moreover, when they are put into practice in teaching large numbers of children in large systems of education, the possible ways of applying them effectively and fairly begin to narrow.

claims.text: An outmoded tool-conception of education has caused low average achievement and a high correlation between social class and educational success in the U.S.
claims.source_passage: An outmoded tool-conception of education and a failure to exploit the cumulative nature of learning have contributed to two shortcomings of American K–12 schooling—a low average level of achievement and a too-exact correlation between social class and educational level.

claims.text: International achievement gaps are reduced when educational systems exploit the cumulative character of learning.
claims.source_passage: international analyses have shown that average levels of achievement across social classes are greatly enhanced and the gap between them reduced when educational systems exploit the cumulative character of learning.

claims.text: The achievement gap between American students and those in other nations, as well as the gap between social classes, widens at each successive grade level due to a failure to exploit the cumulative principle.
claims.source_passage: Our failure to exploit and compensate for this cumulative principle accounts for the growing achievement gap between our students and those in other lands, and for the fairness gap between social classes that widens at each successive grade level in American schools.

claims.text: The widening achievement gap in the U.S. is partially caused by the discouragement felt by students as they fall behind.
claims.source_passage: The widening gap is also the result of discouragement in the have-nots as they witness themselves falling behind.

claims.text: Small early deficits in intellectual capital build into insuperable achievement gaps within a few years of schooling.
claims.source_passage: Many of the most desperately needed policy reforms in American education need to be based on the principle that small early deficits or advantages in intellectual capital build to insuperable gaps after just a few years of schooling.

claims.text: Effective educational policy must be developed coherently across the entire range of schooling, starting from preschool.
claims.source_passage: To take cumulativeness explicitly into account means that educational policy has to be developed coherently over the whole range of a child’s schooling from preschool on

claims.text: The 'ready to learn' principle requires that children enter first grade sharing a minimum threshold of knowledge, vocabulary, and skills with their peers.
claims.source_passage: In short, being “ready to learn” means, at a minimum, sharing knowledge, vocabulary, and skills with other members of the first-grade community.

claims.text: The 1989 education summit's goal that all first graders enter school 'ready to learn' implicitly acknowledges that many children currently enter school unprepared.
claims.source_passage: This goal implicitly acknowledges that many first graders do not now enter school properly prepared.

claims.text: Taking the 'ready to learn' goal seriously implies a massive effort to create truly effective preschool programs.
claims.source_passage: If taken seriously, the goal would imply a massive effort to create truly effective preschool programs.

claims.text: Children cannot keep up in first grade unless they arrive with the specific knowledge, vocabulary, and skills that enable them to participate actively in class.
claims.source_passage: Also implicit in the ready-to-learn principle is the correct assumption that children cannot keep up in first grade unless they arrive there with the knowledge, vocabulary, and skill that will enable them to participate actively in the class and gain further learnings.

claims.text: Logical consistency and democratic principles require extending the 'ready to learn' principle to all subsequent grades, not just first grade.
claims.source_passage: But since the underlying assumption that momentum for all will be sustained is demonstrably false in our current educational system, logical consistency requires that we extend the readiness-to-learn principle to second graders and third graders, and so on.

claims.text: It is the duty of schools in a democracy to provide each child with the knowledge and skills required for academic progress to compensate for unequal home environments.
claims.source_passage: But precisely for that reason, it is the duty of schools in a democracy to compensate and to provide each child with the knowledge and skills requisite for academic progress, regardless of home background.

claims.text: Defining educational standards in multiyear units (e.g., K–4) is an insufficient preliminary step because readiness operates grade by grade.
claims.source_passage: Perhaps in order to avoid appearing arbitrarily prescriptive, these official groups have defined these standards in multiyear grade units such as K–4, 5–8, and 9–12. This is at best a preliminary step. The readiness-to-learn principle operates grade by grade.

claims.text: Effective educational policy requires annual monitoring and compensatory learning for students who fall below the readiness plateau for their upcoming grade.
claims.source_passage: From the standpoint of effective policy, the readiness-to-learn principle must be an annual one, requiring some degree of yearly monitoring and compensatory learning for those who may have drifted below the readiness plateau for the upcoming grade.

claims.text: Grade-by-grade monitoring requires grade-by-grade accountability and incentives for parents, children, teachers, schools, and districts.
claims.source_passage: The policy implication of such grade-by-grade monitoring must be the introduction of grade-by-grade accountability and incentives for everyone concerned with schooling: parents, the children themselves, teachers, schools, districts.

claims.text: Without clear and specific definitions of grade-level readiness, it is impossible to monitor and rectify educational deficits in a timely way.
claims.source_passage: Without clear and specific definitions of what, for example, readiness for second grade means, it is not possible to monitor and rectify deficits in a timely way.

claims.text: Multiyear standards create a system where no specific participant is held responsible for a child's academic shortcomings.
claims.source_passage: Under our current arrangements, and under those now implied by multiyear standards, there is never a specific point when a child, a teacher, or any other participant is responsible for a shortcoming.

claims.text: Only a school system that specifically defines the knowledge and skills required for each grade can be excellent and fair for all students.
claims.source_passage: The inference is unavoidable that only a school system which specifically defines the knowledge and skill required to participate effectively in each successive grade can be excellent and fair for all students.

claims.text: A carefully sequenced and cumulative core of selective content is more meaningful and interesting than a fragmented curriculum.
claims.source_passage: This multiyear core of selective content, being carefully sequenced and cumulative, can be much more meaningful and interesting than a fragmented curriculum.

claims.text: None of the characteristic objections to tests, specific year-by-year goals, or incentives have proved to be valid or supported by good research.
claims.source_passage: In negotiating these accountability arrangements, the community should be aware that none of the characteristic objections to tests, or to specific year-by-year goals, or to incentives have proved to be valid objections supported by good research.

claims.text: Tests, incentives, and accountability are necessary components of a fair and effective educational system.
claims.source_passage: On the contrary, the best research, such as that by John Bishop of Cornell and Harold Stevenson of Michigan, has shown that tests, incentives, and accountability are necessary to a fair and effective system.

claims.text: The notions that children can progress through school at their own innate pace are egregious fallacies unsupported by research.
claims.source_passage: The notions that children can progress through school at their own pace and that teachers are in any position to decide what a child’s innate pace actually is are egregious fallacies unsupported by research.

claims.text: There is no natural pace for gaining the nonnatural learnings of alphabetic literacy and base-ten mathematics.
claims.source_passage: There is no natural pace for gaining the nonnatural learnings of alphabetic literacy and base-ten mathematics.

claims.text: It is impossible to conduct an effective classroom when attempting to accommodate twenty-five different paces of learning simultaneously.
claims.source_passage: Moreover, it is impossible to conduct an effective classroom when there are attempts to accommodate twenty-five different “paces” of learning.

claims.text: The best classrooms bring all students along at a slow but sure rate while providing compensatory instruction as needed.
claims.source_passage: The best classrooms bring all students along at a slow but sure rate, monitoring success for all at each step, providing compensatory instruction as needed, and, in addition, giving talented and eager students challenging extra work to do.

claims.text: Unstandardized tests are inherently unfair because they are scored differently by different people on different occasions.
claims.source_passage: Unstandardized tests—that is, tests that are scored differently by different people on different occasions—are inherently unfair

claims.text: Grade-by-grade standards and tests are logically necessary for monitoring and attaining grade-by-grade readiness.
claims.source_passage: In short, grade-by-grade standards and some form of fair grade-by-grade tests are logically necessary for monitoring and attaining grade-by-grade readiness.

claims.text: Education schools fail to instruct prospective teachers about the best research into effective pedagogical methods.
claims.source_passage: Popham showed that because of the failure to instruct prospective teachers about the best research into effective pedagogical methods (the findings of which happen to contravene the naturalistic approaches that continue to be advocated), uncertified persons, plopped into the classroom without having taken education courses, got results that were as good as those obtained by certified and experienced teachers.

claims.text: Naturalistic principles like discovery learning and individual pacing do not work well according to existing research.
claims.source_passage: The existing research says that these methods do not work well.

claims.text: The best way to develop skills or knowledge is to focus on that specific domain and monitor whether it has been gained.
claims.source_passage: The best way to develop a skill or a domain of knowledge in a group of students is to focus (by any number of agreeable and interesting methods) on that skill or that domain, and to monitor whether that skill or that knowledge has in fact been gained.

claims.text: External incentives combined with intrinsic ones work better than intrinsic incentives alone.
claims.source_passage: The research also shows that external incentives combined with intrinsic ones work better than intrinsic incentives alone.

claims.text: An accomplishment-through-diligence mentality is more productive than a learning-is-easy-and-joyful mentality.
claims.source_passage: It shows that skill and knowledge do not come without effort, even to talented students, suggesting that an accomplishment-through-diligence mentality is more productive than a learning-is-easy-and-joyful mentality

claims.text: The pandemic of mistaken ideas in education schools is the gravest barrier to America's educational improvement.
claims.source_passage: The resulting pandemic of mistaken ideas may be the gravest barrier to America’s educational improvement.

claims.text: Teacher certification should be dependent on demonstrating subject-matter knowledge and knowledge of research-based pedagogies.
claims.source_passage: Make teacher certification dependent upon prospective teachers’ demonstrating adequate, probing knowledge of the subject matters they will he called upon to teach, as well as adequate knowledge of mainstream research into the most effective pedagogies for imparting those subject matters.

claims.text: Millions of American teachers are offered no intellectual alternatives to the repeating mistakes of progressive educational doctrine.
claims.source_passage: consisting of millions of teachers, are offered no intellectual alternatives to these constantly repeated mistakes, which are, indeed, presented as fruits of the most recent research.

claims.text: If progressive indoctrination in teacher training continues, the system for certifying and training teachers must be revolutionized.
claims.source_passage: If this disabling indoctrination continues unabated, then something quite revolutionary will have to be done about our system of training and certifying teachers.

claims.text: Reform must begin by insisting on intellectual diversity within schools of education.
claims.source_passage: A beginning can be made by insisting upon more intellectual diversity within education schools.

claims.text: Progressive educational doctrine cannot withstand empirical and intellectual challenge in a free and open encounter.
claims.source_passage: The tenacity and unanimity with which they adhere to the progressive doctrine may be owing to that doctrine’s inability to withstand empirical and intellectual challenge in a free and open encounter.

claims.text: A small group of dissenting professors in each education school could potentially dismantle the current educational establishment.
claims.source_passage: If such is the case, as I believe, then perhaps a rather small cadre of maverick professors in every education school might soon make the whole Wizard of Oz apparatus collapse like a punctured balloon.

claims.text: Every nation that achieves universal grade-level readiness for all children does so by using grade-by-grade standards.
claims.source_passage: Every nation that manages to achieve universal readiness in the early grades for all its children—a few examples are France, Hungary, Norway, Japan, Korea, Sweden, and Denmark—does so by following grade-by-grade standards.

claims.text: A common core curriculum is the only practical way for large, diverse nations to achieve universal student readiness.
claims.source_passage: In large, diverse nations as well as small, homogeneous ones, a common core curriculum appears to be the only practical means for achieving universal readiness at each grade level.

claims.text: Universal readiness is the only means for achieving universal competence and combining educational excellence with fairness.
claims.source_passage: Universal readiness, in turn, is the only means for achieving universal competence and for combining excellence with fairness.

claims.text: No nation that lacks grade-by-grade standards has achieved universal readiness, excellence, and fairness simultaneously.
claims.source_passage: In contrast, no nation that dispenses with grade-by-grade standards has managed to achieve universal readiness, excellence, and fairness.

claims.text: The Netherlands fails to achieve educational fairness and universal competence because it lacks a core curriculum.
claims.source_passage: The Netherlands, for example, is a non-core-curriculum nation that has managed to attain a high average level of excellence because of very high achievement among the top half of its students, but it has failed to achieve universal competence and fairness.

claims.text: Switzerland's high achievement is actually due to detailed grade-by-grade standards at the local (canton) level, not a lack of standards.
claims.source_passage: But on closer inspection, we find that in fact Switzerland has one of the most detailed and demanding core curriculums in the world, with each canton specifying in detail the minimum knowledge and skill that each child shall achieve in each grade.

claims.text: Democracy requires a common culture so that citizens can discuss collective problems using common meanings.
claims.source_passage: The first... is the need of a democracy for many common elements in the culture of all the people, to the end that the people may discuss collective problems in terms that will convey common meanings.

claims.text: The lack of curricular uniformity in the United States is a gross injustice to millions of highly mobile school children.
claims.source_passage: Under these conditions, failure to have a goodly measure of uniformity in school subjects and grade placement is a gross injustice to at least ten million school children at the present time.

claims.text: High student mobility in the United States makes common grade-by-grade learning standards more necessary than in other nations.
claims.source_passage: In sum, the high mobility of our children, especially of those who can least afford educational disruption, makes common learnings more needed in the United States than in most other nations.

claims.text: One sixth of all American third graders attend at least three different schools between first and third grade.
claims.source_passage: General Accounting Office reported that one sixth of all third graders attend at least three schools between first and third grade.

claims.text: American diversity, size, and nomadism are logical arguments in favor of, rather than against, common grade-by-grade standards.
claims.source_passage: Our diversity, size, and nomadism are arguments in favor of, not against, common, grade-by-grade standards.

claims.text: Educational localism in the United States is a quasi-sacred principle that rarely results in the imposition of explicit content standards by local authorities.
claims.source_passage: Localism remains a quasi-sacred principle, despite the fact that few localities actually impose explicit content standards.

claims.text: Common learnings are a necessary prerequisite for a functioning democracy.
claims.source_passage: Bagley’s other point, that common learnings are necessary to a functioning democracy, is an educational principle that has been accepted in most democracies of the world, including our own in its earlier years.

claims.text: A citizenry cannot participate effectively in a modern economy without sharing the common intellectual capital that enables communication.
claims.source_passage: A citizenry cannot read and understand newspapers, much less participate effectively in a modern economy, without sharing the common intellectual capital that makes understanding and communication possible.

claims.text: In a large and diverse nation, the common school is the only institution capable of creating a school-based culture that enables universal public communication.
claims.source_passage: In a large, diverse nation, the common school is the only institution available for creating a school-based culture that, like a common language, enables everyone to communicate in the public sphere.

claims.text: The principle of shared background knowledge in the classroom is the same principle that allows an entire community or nation to function.
claims.source_passage: The principle that children should enter a new grade already sharing the background knowledge required to understand the teacher and each other is at bottom the principle that enables the functioning of an entire community or nation.

claims.text: It is the duty of a nation’s educational system to create a domain of public communicability.
claims.source_passage: It is the duty of a nation’s educational system to create this domain of public communicability.

claims.text: The American founders intended for divisiveness, specifically sectarian religion, to be excluded from the cultural commons of the public sphere.
claims.source_passage: Divisiveness was to be excluded from the cultural commons. Guided by the principle of public toleration enunciated by John Locke and others... the founders desired that the laws and customs of the public sphere should favor no single sect but should promote the general welfare.

claims.text: The separation of church and state was an Enlightenment innovation designed to foster a public domain of toleration and civility while preserving private freedom.
claims.source_passage: The deliberately artificial wall of separation helped create and nurture a public domain of toleration and civility, while leaving everyone as free as possible in their private lives. It was a brilliant Enlightenment political innovation for encouraging internal peace and solidarity in a large nation.

claims.text: Ethnic sectarianism is the primary contemporary threat to ecumenical, cosmopolitan public culture, mirroring the historical threat of religious sectarianism.
claims.source_passage: In our own day, the chief danger to this ecumenical, cosmopolitan public culture is not a religious but an ethnic sectarianism.

claims.text: American public culture is a hybrid construct containing heterogeneous elements from various ethnic groups.
claims.source_passage: so our public culture is a hybrid construct that contains heterogeneous elements from various ethnic groups.

claims.text: Ethnic sectarianism is the chief danger to the shared public culture, functioning similarly to religious sectarianism in its divisiveness.
claims.source_passage: The chief danger to this ecumenical, cosmopolitan public culture is not a religious but an ethnic sectarianism. The two kinds of sect, religious and ethnic, are highly similar in their divisiveness and their danger to the shared public sphere.

claims.text: Universities and businesses should prioritize integration and the furtherance of an ecumenical national culture over the promotion of diversity for its own right.
claims.source_passage: Universities and businesses should return to the principle of integration, to the notion that diversity is not something to be celebrated and promoted in its own right, but an opportunity for mutual understanding and the furtherance of an ecumenical national culture.

claims.text: Multiculturalism should be guided into an ecumenical, cosmopolitan direction rather than fostering angry separatism.
claims.source_passage: Patterson and others, notably Arthur Schlesinger, Jr., have been urging that the principle of multiculturalism should be guided into this ecumenical, cosmopolitan direction for the good of the nation rather than fostering its all-too-prevalent tendency toward angry separatism and mutual hostility.

claims.text: The Romantic version of ethnicity is as harmful to public education as the Romantic conception of pedagogy.
claims.source_passage: In my view, the Romantic version of ethnicity is as deleterious to public education as the Romantic conception of pedagogy.

claims.text: Common school learnings should promote a cosmopolitan, ecumenical, hybrid public culture where all citizens meet on an equal footing.
claims.source_passage: The common learnings taught in school should promote a cosmopolitan, ecumenical, hybrid public culture in which all meet on an equal footing—a culture that is as deliberately artificial and non-sectarian as our public invocations of the Divinity.

claims.text: A specific common core of content is currently repellent to many Americans.
claims.source_passage: A highly specific common core of content is still repellent to many Americans.

claims.text: Educational formalism has turned the principle of local curriculum control into a myth.
claims.source_passage: The public will be all the more likely to reach this conclusion when it becomes more fully aware that educational formalism has turned the jealously guarded principle of local curriculum control into a myth.

claims.text: As far as specific content is concerned, the local curriculum does not exist in most cases.
claims.source_passage: As far as specific content is concerned, the local curriculum, with few exceptions, does not exist.

claims.text: Focusing on K-8 education will inherently improve American high schools by providing students with foundational knowledge.
claims.source_passage: If the principles of early education advanced here were to be followed, American high schools would change perforce for the better. Their incoming students would have already received the foundational knowledge and skill needed for good citizenship.

claims.text: High schools would become more effective if students were not required to take so many remedial/elementary courses.
claims.source_passage: Students would not need to be shepherded into so many elementary courses; they could follow more varied and intensive strands of academic or vocational study according to their interests and abilities.

claims.text: Schools require a coherent, cumulative core curriculum that instills consensus values such as civic duty, honesty, diligence, and independent-mindedness.
claims.source_passage: Schools need to have a coherent, cumulative core curriculum which instills consensus values such as civic duty, honesty, diligence, perseverance, respect, kindness, and independent-mindedness

claims.text: Educational accountability must apply to students, teachers, schools, and parents regarding specific year-by-year goals.
claims.source_passage: and which holds students, teachers, schools, and parents accountable for acceptable progress in achieving these specific year-by-year goals.

claims.text: Effective schools must provide step-by-step mastery of procedural knowledge in language arts and mathematics alongside content knowledge in civics, science, the arts, and the humanities.
claims.source_passage: which gives students step-by-step mastery of procedural knowledge in language arts and mathematics; which gives them step-by-step mastery of content knowledge in civics, science, the arts, and the humanities

claims.text: Providing parents with detailed outlines of specific knowledge goals enables better monitoring of a child's progress.
claims.source_passage: To this end, we provide parents with a detailed outline of the specific knowledge and skill goals for each grade, and we stay in constant touch with them regarding the child’s progress.

claims.text: A knowledge-based approach ensures that all children with normal abilities perform at grade level while still challenging talented children.
claims.source_passage: Through this knowledge-based approach, we make sure that all normal children perform at grade level, while, in addition, the most talented children are challenged to excel.

claims.text: Attaining specific and well-integrated knowledge provides students with pleasure in learning and self-respect.
claims.source_passage: Attaining this specific and well-integrated knowledge and skill gives our students pleasure in learning, as well as self-respect

claims.text: Parental choice and charter school policies are effective insofar as they liberate individual initiative to implement high standards of skill and knowledge.
claims.source_passage: the kind of initiative greatly facilitated by large-scale policies that consciously liberate individual initiative, policies such as “parental choice” and “charter schools,” which give parents and teachers the power to change their own individual schools, so long as their students are trained to high standards of skill and knowledge.

claims.text: Romantic progressivism has maintained virtually totalitarian intellectual dominion over education schools and policymakers for nearly seventy years.
claims.source_passage: It has taken nearly seventy years for Romantic progressivism to exercise virtually totalitarian intellectual dominion over not just schools of education but a large percentage of policymakers and the general public as well.

claims.text: No effective large-scale policy change is possible until there is a fundamental change of mind regarding education among the general public and teachers.
claims.source_passage: Nothing truly effective in the way of large-scale policy change—through federal, state, or local mechanisms—can be accomplished, no new power relationships can be forged, until there is a change of mind by the general public—among whom I include two and a half million teachers.

claims.text: There is no real-world evidence that having common elements in a school curriculum leads to social uniformity or creates 'interchangeable automatons.'
claims.source_passage: There is no evidence whatever that this fear of uniformity, which is widespread and often expressed, has any real-world foundation, or that a moiety of commonality in the school curriculum will turn everyone into interchangeable automatons.

claims.text: The current laissez-faire curricular approach relies on a misguided Romantic faith in the 'watchful beneficence of nature.'
claims.source_passage: The foundation for this curricular confidence (which has in fact resulted in huge knowledge gaps, boring repetitions, and glaring inequalities) would seem to be a Romantic faith in the watchful beneficence of nature

claims.text: The laissez-faire curricular model has resulted in huge knowledge gaps, boring repetitions, and glaring inequalities in education.
claims.source_passage: The foundation for this curricular confidence (which has in fact resulted in huge knowledge gaps, boring repetitions, and glaring inequalities)

claims.text: Enhancing the content and commonality of education would diminish economic inequities within the nation.
claims.source_passage: The improvement would, as everyone knows, diminish the economic inequities within the nation.

claims.text: There has been a decline in civility and sense of community in American public life, characterized by interethnic hostilities and an us-versus-them mentality.
claims.source_passage: Many observers have deplored the decline in civility in our public life, and with it the decline in our sense of community. The interethnic hostilities that have intensified among us recently, the development of an us-versus-them mentality in political life

claims.text: The idea that scholarly attainments are determined naturally and follow an innate course of development is a fallacy that harms educational effectiveness.
claims.source_passage: the pace and quality of each child’s scholarly attainments are determined naturally, and will follow an innate course of development which should not be interfered with by external impositions of drills and hard work.

claims.text: Enhancing both the content and commonality of education improves its effectiveness and fairness.
claims.source_passage: Improving the effectiveness and fairness of education through enhancing both its content and its commonality has a more than educational significance.

claims.text: Economic inequities in the United States have increased significantly in recent decades.
claims.source_passage: Nothing could be more important to our national well-being than overcoming those inequities, which have grown ever greater in recent decades.

claims.text: Interethnic hostilities in the United States have intensified in recent years.
claims.source_passage: The interethnic hostilities that have intensified among us recently, the development of an us-versus-them mentality in political life, the astonishing indifference to the condition of our children—all bespeak a decline in the communitarian spirit

claims.text: The 'communitarian spirit' was previously a defining characteristic of American national culture.
claims.source_passage: the communitarian spirit, which used to be a hallmark of what Patterson calls our “ecumenical national culture.”

claims.text: A truly common school strengthens universal communicability within the public sphere.
claims.source_passage: But an equally important contribution of the truly common school would be the strengthening of universal communicability and a sense of community within the public sphere.

claims.text: The most significant contribution of common schooling is the preservation of democracy through shared communication.
claims.source_passage: In the long run, that could be the common school’s most important contribution to preserving the fragile fabric of our democracy.

claims.text: The fact that progressivism originated at Teachers College, Columbia University in the 1920s is a well-established historical observation supported by scholars like Laurence Cremin and Diane Ravitch.
claims.source_passage: To provide historical perspective, the book has devoted a number of pages to showing that the direct origin of our dominant (and ineffectual) educational orientation was the progressive movement of the 1920s that emanated from Teachers College, Columbia University. That is not of course a new observation, as any reader of Laurence Cremin or Diane Ravitch can attest.

claims.text: Romanticism is arguably superior to the Enlightenment in the sphere of poetry, even if it is inferior in the sphere of education.
claims.source_passage: In the sphere of poetry, Romanticism is arguably superior to the Enlightenment, but in the sphere of education, Romantic principles have proved to be much less valid than Enlightenment ones as guides to practice.

claims.text: The press and the public have a moral and civic obligation to reject the argument that progressive methods have 'never been properly tried'.
claims.source_passage: It is incumbent upon the public and the press to reject this “never-properly-tried” argument. We simply cannot afford more decades of “child-centered” credulity.

claims.text: Half-truths are the most dangerous forms of error because their plausibility makes them difficult to recognize as incorrect.
claims.source_passage: Both formalism and naturalism are half-truths—the most pernicious kinds of errors because they appear so plausible.

claims.text: The automation of repeated cognitive operations is the functional prerequisite that enables the conscious mind to engage in critical thought.
claims.source_passage: It is true that certain operations of reading are the same from one task to another, so that expertness in reading partly depends upon the automation (through a great deal of practice) of those repeated operations, thus freeing the conscious mind for critical thought.

claims.text: Working memory is a physiological constraint that requires the mind to 'strike while the iron is hot' or lose information permanently.
claims.source_passage: The mind must strike while the iron is hot; if it doesn’t, those vanished elements will cease to be available. For overcoming these limitations of working memory, the mind uses rules and schemas to achieve automation and chunking.

claims.text: The 'democratic version' of the cumulative principle requires that every student be guaranteed the academic preparation necessary to succeed in their specific grade level.
claims.source_passage: The democratic version of the cumulative principle demands that every student who enters a class at the beginning of the year shall be vouchsafed the academic preparation needed to gain the knowledge and skills to be taught during that year.

claims.text: Multi-year standards (e.g., K-4) are effectively unmonitored systems because they fail to assign responsibility for a child's academic deficit to a specific point in time or participant.
claims.source_passage: The multigrade approach to accountability is essentially an unmonitored system that offers no fair or enforceable incentives for any participant, including the child.

claims.text: The success of the Swiss educational system is predicated on low student mobility, as children there rarely move between cantons.
claims.source_passage: But on closer inspection, we find that in fact Switzerland has one of the most detailed and demanding core curriculums in the world... but equally important, Swiss children rarely move from one canton to another in the course of their schooling.

claims.text: The 'wall of separation' between church and state was a deliberately artificial innovation intended to foster a public domain of toleration while leaving private lives free.
claims.source_passage: The deliberately artificial wall of separation helped create and nurture a public domain of toleration and civility, while leaving everyone as free as possible in their private lives. It was a brilliant Enlightenment political innovation for encouraging internal peace and solidarity in a large nation.

claims.text: The dogmatism and lack of intellectual diversity in education schools may be a defensive reaction to the inability of progressive doctrine to survive empirical challenge.
claims.source_passage: The tenacity and unanimity with which they adhere to the progressive doctrine may be owing to that doctrine’s inability to withstand empirical and intellectual challenge in a free and open encounter.

evidence.description: Historical account of the origin of American progressive education at Teachers College, Columbia University.
evidence.source_reference: Cremin, The Transformation of the School; Ravitch, The Troubled Crusade

evidence.description: Meta-analysis/summary of process-outcome research showing progressivist methods are the least effective.
evidence.source_reference: Rosenshine and Stevens, 'Teaching Functions' (1986); Brophy and Good (1986)

evidence.description: Emerson's 1839 journal entry expressing the view that formal schooling is less effective than learning on a farm.
The 'biscuit-making' example in an Underground Railroad project showing how naturalistic methods lead to irrelevant learning.
Process-outcome research showing that direct instruction is more effective than naturalistic settings for universal learning.
evidence.source_reference: Rosenshine and Stevens, 'Teaching Functions'; Brophy and Good, 'Teacher Behavior and Student Achievement'

evidence.description: Psychological findings on the 'domain-specific' nature of intellectual skills.
evidence.source_reference: Larkin and Chabay, 'Research on Teaching Scientific Thinking'

evidence.description: Psychological research demonstrating that skills like critical thinking are 'domain-specific' and do not easily transfer.
evidence.source_reference: Larkin and Chabay, “Research on Teaching Scientific Thinking,” 150–72.
evidence.source_passage: Psychologists have found that this formalistic model does not work in the real world. It represents the slow, unreliable approach of amateurs and novices rather than the knowledge-informed approach of experts.

evidence.description: Scientific consensus that native-language learning is biological and mediated by specific brain locations.
evidence.source_reference: Pinker, The Language Instinct; Geary, 'Reflections of Evolution and Culture in Children’s Cognition'
evidence.source_passage: Scientific opinion has converged on the view that native-language learning is mediated by dedicated locations in the brain, and is as inexorable and inherent as physical development.

evidence.description: The difference in language proficiency between advantaged and disadvantaged toddlers based on parental stimulation.
evidence.source_reference: Implicitly refers to language development research (Hart & Risley style, though not named here).

evidence.description: Thought experiment regarding the inability to read about the Civil War without relevant vocabulary, even if one has 'reading skills'.
The historical rarity of alphabetic literacy compared to the universality of oral language.
California's decline in national reading scores following the implementation of whole-language instruction.
International math score comparisons showing US students at the bottom.
David Geary's studies on evolution and culture in children's cognition regarding mathematical development.
evidence.source_reference: Geary, David, 'Reflections of Evolution and Culture in Children’s Cognition'

evidence.description: David Geary's research distinguishing between innate (primary) and noninnate (secondary) cognitive learnings.
evidence.source_reference: Geary, “Reflections of Evolution and Culture in Children’s Cognition,” 1995
evidence.source_passage: One of the most useful lines of research in recent years has concerned innate and noninnate learnings

evidence.description: Steven Pinker's work on the biological basis of language acquisition.
evidence.source_reference: Pinker, The Language Instinct
evidence.source_passage: Steven Pinker has shown that children are born with a “language instinct.”

evidence.description: Thompson's findings on the plasticity of the young mind and ease of secondary learning at early ages.
evidence.source_reference: Thompson, The Brain, 299–333
evidence.source_passage: After age five, the plasticity of the young mind makes many secondary learnings easier and more durable at early ages.

evidence.description: Studies on the 'distributed practice' effect for memory retention.
evidence.source_reference: Bahrick, “Extending the Life Span of Knowledge,” 1984
evidence.source_passage: Memory studies indicate that the best approach to achieving retention in long-term memory is “distributed practice,” with rehearsal occurring at moderately distant intervals.

evidence.description: Cognitive science models of schema theory applied to mathematical development.
evidence.source_reference: Geary, Children’s Mathematical Development
evidence.source_passage: The current consensus is that these competencies are described, at least operationally, by schema theory

evidence.description: Chess expertise research shows that established schemas reduce the number of new elements a mind must attend to.
Students of logic are only marginally more logical than those who haven't taken the subject, demonstrating the failure of formal-procedure transfer.
Walberg and Tsai's research on 'Matthew Effects' in education regarding cumulative advantages.
evidence.source_reference: Walberg and Tsai, 'Matthew Effects in Education' (1983)

evidence.description: Historical expert testimony from Euclid to King Ptolemy regarding the cumulative nature of learning.
Research on chess expertise demonstrating how possessed schemas prevent short-term memory overload.
International analyses showing that systems exploiting cumulative learning enhance achievement and reduce class gaps.
The educational system of France, which recognizes that early knowledge deficits must be overcome quickly.
The 'Matthew effect' in educational research, describing the widening gap between those with and without knowledge.
evidence.source_reference: Walberg and Tsai, 'Matthew Effects in Education'

evidence.description: The 1989 education summit where President Bush and forty-nine governors set the 'ready to learn' goal.
The consensus of the 1989 education summit regarding school readiness.
Research on the necessity of incentives and accountability for educational fairness.
evidence.source_reference: John Bishop (Cornell)

evidence.description: Cross-cultural research on mathematics achievement and systemic factors.
evidence.source_reference: Harold Stevenson (Michigan)

evidence.description: Studies showing that curriculum-based examinations and accountability systems increase learning and incentives across various countries.
evidence.source_reference: John Bishop, 'Why U.S. Students Need Incentives to Learn' (1991), 'Impacts of School Organization...'

evidence.description: Comparative research on mathematics achievement between Asian and American children emphasizing the role of standards and effort.
evidence.source_reference: Stevenson and Stigler, The Learning Gap (1992)

evidence.description: Study showing uncertified teachers perform as well as certified ones because education schools fail to teach effective pedagogy.
evidence.source_reference: W. James Popham, 'Performance Tests of Teaching Proficiency' (1971)

evidence.description: Comparative success of France, Hungary, Norway, Japan, Korea, Sweden, and Denmark in achieving universal readiness via grade-by-grade standards.
evidence.source_reference: International Association for the Evaluation of Educational Achievement, Science Achievement in Seventeen Countries

evidence.description: The Netherlands exhibits the lowest degree of educational fairness in Northern Europe, with 16% of schools below minimum competency compared to 2.5% in core-curriculum neighbors.
evidence.source_reference: International Association for the Evaluation of Educational Achievement, Science Achievement in Seventeen Countries

evidence.description: Switzerland achieves the best combination of excellence and fairness in the world due to canton-level core curricula and low student mobility.
evidence.source_reference: Lapointe, Mead, and Askew, Learning Mathematics and Learning Science

evidence.description: Inner-city student mobility rates in the U.S. routinely range from 45% to over 100% per year.
evidence.source_reference: Cohen, 'Moving Images'

evidence.description: GAO report stating one-sixth of all U.S. third graders attend at least three schools between first and third grade.
evidence.source_reference: General Accounting Office, Elementary School Children, 1

evidence.description: Report showing high rates of student mobility in the US, with 1/6th of third graders attending 3+ schools.
evidence.source_reference: General Accounting Office, Elementary School Children, 1
evidence.source_passage: General Accounting Office reported that one sixth of all third graders attend at least three schools between first and third grade.

evidence.description: Jefferson's assertion that a functioning democracy (symbolized by newspapers) requires literate and knowledgeable citizens.
evidence.source_reference: Jefferson, The Life and Selected Writings of Thomas Jefferson, 126
evidence.source_passage: When Jefferson said that if he had to choose between newspapers and government, he would choose newspapers, he went on to say that his remark was premised on each citizen’s being able to read and understand the newspapers.

evidence.description: Orlando Patterson's concept of 'ecumenical America' as a multi-ethnic overarching national culture.
evidence.source_reference: Patterson, 'Affirmative Action on the Merit System,' A-13
evidence.source_passage: Recently, Orlando Patterson has spoken of 'cross-pollinating our multi-ethnic communities' in order to 'promote that precious overarching national culture—the envy of the world—which I call ecumenical America.'

evidence.description: The acceptance of a 50 percent common core curriculum by parents in Core Knowledge schools.
Observation of ethnic separatism and hostility on large university campuses.
Historical development of national languages from hybrid medieval lingua francas.
Historical precedent of citizens becoming successful by grade eight in earlier eras.
Observation of current educational outcomes characterized by knowledge gaps and inequalities under the dominant progressive model.
The author's own 'school-by-school grassroots effort' (Core Knowledge Foundation) as a model for how ideas can spread from individual successful schools.
Orlando Patterson's characterization of American culture as an 'ecumenical national culture' marked by communitarian spirit.
evidence.source_reference: Patterson, 'Affirmative Action on the Merit System'

concepts.author_definition: A pervasive cultural orientation emphasizing child-centered, natural development, and internal motivation, while disparaging focused instruction and 'mere' verbal information.
A rhetorical defense for failed progressive policies which claims that failure is due to inauthentic implementation rather than flawed theory.
concepts.common_alternative: The idea that progressive education failed because it lacked adequate funding or teacher training.

concepts.author_definition: An abstract skill often prioritized over factual knowledge, which the author asserts is a myth and does not exist as a general skill.
concepts.common_alternative: Metacognitive skills or 'learning to learn' strategies.

concepts.author_definition: The belief that the particular content learned in school is less important than acquiring formal intellectual tools like 'learning to learn' or 'critical-thinking skills.'
The belief that education is a natural process that works best when it follows the child's inherent rhythms and real-life settings, and that artificial school structures are harmful.
The principle that cognitive abilities (like critical thinking or reading) are tied to specific knowledge of a subject and do not automatically transfer between unrelated fields.
An approach that attempts to teach knowledge and skills in 'real-life' contexts rather than through separate subjects or drill and practice.
A competency based on both automated formal habits and wide-ranging general knowledge that allows for application to new problems.
concepts.source_passage: A general skill... is also based on wide-ranging general knowledge. A broad base of knowledge enables a well-educated person to apply domain-relevant knowledge to a wide range of new problems and experiences—which is the true meaning of “learning to learn.”

concepts.author_definition: The ability to apply broad, domain-relevant knowledge to new experiences, rather than the mastery of content-free strategies.
concepts.common_alternative: Metacognitive strategies or study skills independent of content knowledge.

concepts.author_definition: The educational belief that school learning should mimic natural, innately motivated processes like learning a mother tongue or physiomotor development.
An approach to reading instruction that treats literacy as a 'psycholinguistic guessing game' acquired through exposure to meaningful literature rather than direct instruction in sounds.
